author: @ClownWhisper
text:
Fill it with water, freeze it

Done and done.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Clown Whisper great plan if you have a freezer - i don't have one in the lab - not done and not done

author: @TheIdeanator
text: I'm in the middle of making an induction heater myself and I'm very curious to see your build.

author: @thagrit
text: Hey Robert! Fill the tube with water and freeze it then wind it around your former! I saw another you tuber do that to make a water heater. It works well.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: thagrit works well if you have a freezer mate - i don't have one in the lab - so this works well for me

author: @yamon6135
text: you can freeze the copper full of water  and then bend the coil to keep it from kinking

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Ya Mon only if you have a freezer - i don't have one at the lab

author: @cavemanelectronics9611
text:
damn you work fast when you get down to business!  ;)
i cant wait to see what youve done with all the heat pipes you were working on.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: jeremy gwilt lol - i have to get back to that - but i will

author: @chrisstevens2
text: Hmmm, I wonder if that giant coil will eventually be combined with your supercapacitor.  ;)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: chrisstevens2 lol - am i that transparent lol

replies.author: @TheIdeanator
replies.text: Robert Murray-Smith It won't do induction save for the split second it takes to charge the coil and it won't do an efficient coilgun either. I tried that already with a 40F 18V bank of maxwell caps for my physics final and it did worse than typical electrolytic models do.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: TheIdeanator i wouldn't worry about it mate -  i am not combining it in that way

author: @moto2kx2
text: Made a nice pre-heater for my water on my pellet stove pipe about 35 turns. Worked nicely. Water heater hardly turns on all winter. Fortunately had a piece of steel pipe the right size...

author: @bernardstokes6550
text: nice technique .....just in time for me making my heat exchanger coil for my wood burner .....just a little larger both in pipe and coil diameter . Thanks for sharing and most timely.

author: @planetengineeringofficial8545
text: so your going to video all the parts for it? awesome

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

text: First lol, Nice info. I use a propane torch to warm it a bit before bending coils for boilers. Super cap induction heating eah? ;)
